item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Performance check using the item
markdown:
**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 144 **Price** 8,500 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- Crafted by [archons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=365) for mortal use, this trumpet is made of luminous gold and ivory, giving off a soft glow that warms the soul. This trumpet grants you a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks while playing music with the instrument. **Activate—Archon's Note** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You blast a note on the horn so clear and pure that its grandeur stuns your enemies and inspires your allies. Allies in the area gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls and saving throws for 1 round. Enemies within a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 35 Fortitude saving throw. They’re then temporarily immune for 1 day. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 and off-guard for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is stunned 2 and off-guard for 1 round.
name: Horn of the Archon

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Diplomacy against troops, individual soldiers, and military leaders
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Marshal's Baton](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3941)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 145

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This short, thick, stick-like object is crafted of wood and steel. Precious metals decorate the grip, and fine filigree marks the caps on each end. A _marshal’s baton_ grants you a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) and [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks against troops, individual soldiers, and military leaders.

**Activate—Stentorian Order** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You issue a command in a booming voice while gesturing with the _marshal’s baton_ and cast a [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470) spell (DC 18). This spell affects [troops](/Rules.aspx?ID=3364) and [swarms](/Traits.aspx?ID=707) as if they were a single creature.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Marshal's Baton

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Deception checks to send false signals to enemy forces
markdown:
**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 146 **Price** 1,250 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** L --- This semaphore set consists of two hardwood poles painted white, with finely crafted silk flags bisected by yellow and red fields. A tiny stylized black serpent with its tongue extended is depicted on the top outer corner of each flag. The _semaphore of slanders_, in addition to being a functioning semaphore set, can be used to send false signals to any enemy forces observing the signaler, providing a +2 item bonus to Deception checks to do so, and allies are always aware of this ruse. **Activate—Insidious Insinuation** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You activate the semaphore to mislead the enemy. Choose a creature within 60 feet to attempt a DC 28 Will saving throw. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The target is frightened 1 and is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target is frightened 2 and off-guard for 2 rounds.
name: Semaphore of Slanders

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Performance check using the item
markdown:
**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 146 **Price** 140 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** L --- This ash recorder is highly polished, with a spiral, serpentine pattern etched along its length. The recorder grants you a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks while playing music with the instrument. **Activate—Sooth Serpents** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You play a swift composition that fascinates all snakes, pythons, vipers, and serpents that hear it. At the GM’s discretion, creatures with major serpentine features, such as [serpentfolk](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=468), are also subjected to this effect. All such creatures within a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 20 Will save. **Critical Success** The target’s attitude toward you decreases by one step.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The target’s attitude toward you improves by one step, and it feels a powerful urge to dance. It takes a –5- foot penalty to its Speeds for 1 minute as it sways and dances to the music.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target’s attitude toward you improves by two steps.
name: Sinuous Recorder

markdown:
**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 147 **Price** 7,000 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 1 --- This snare drum and sticks are made from dark wood, and the skin is fine antelope hide. This drum grants you a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks while playing music with the instrument. **Activate—Prestissimo** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You play the snare with an extreme cadence. You and all allies within a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) gain the [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) condition until the end of your next turn and can use the extra action each round for only [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) and [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) actions. **Activate—Larghissimo** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You play the snare with a lassitude that drains the speed from your foes. Enemies within a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 34 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is slowed 2 for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is slowed 2 and [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round.
name: Snare of Speed

markdown:
**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 147 **Price** 650 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 1 --- This fine copper kettledrum has a dark skin stretched over it, and the tension rods are stained a dark red. This drum grants you a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks while playing music with the instrument. **Activate—Sustain Dread** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You beat a march on the timpani that continuously increases in tempo. Enemies within a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 26 Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The target can’t reduce its [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) value below 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** The target can’t reduce its frightened value below 1 for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and it becomes frightened 1.
name: Timpani of Panic

<traits>

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

This finely crafted set of bagpipes bears the hallmark scratches and wear of the battlefield, but it nonetheless shines with polish and has been played with love. These bagpipes grant you a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks while playing music with the instrument.

**Activate—Inspirational Salute** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You tap into the great music of the pipes, inspiring all allies who can hear. You and all allies within a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) gain a +1 status bonus to damage rolls and saves against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) effects for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Warpipes

[Wyvern Nafir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3952)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 147

**Price** 950 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This simple trumpet is carved from a single wyvern horn. A _wyvern nafir_ grants you a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks while playing music with the instrument.

**Activate—Wyvern Scream** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You blast a draconic scream from the nafir. All creatures in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) take 5d10 sonic damage (DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). On a failed save, the target is pushed back 5 feet (or 10 feet on a critical failure).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wyvern Nafir

>
[Admiral's Bicorne](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3954)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 148

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ostentatious hat is trimmed with gold thread and tiny jewels, all proclaiming your position of authority on the high seas. While wearing the bicorne, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) and Sailing [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) checks.

**Activate—Fight On** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You take damage from an enemy’s [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) or spell attack roll;  **Effect** Despite your wounds, your troops are inspired to fight on. For 1 minute, all allies in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) gain a +2 status bonus to saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) effects.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Admiral's Bicorne

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Grippy Gloves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3966)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 150

**Price** 90 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A good pair of gloves is a critical item for any soldier, particularly if you plan to get up close to your enemy. When you wear these black leather gloves with fine silver stitching, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376), and [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379).

**Activate—Sticky Grip** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You have an enemy g[rabbed ](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77)or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effect** Your gloves help you squeeze even more tightly, keeping your opponent from moving freely. The enemy you have grabbed or restrained is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Grippy Gloves

    right="Item 4+"

>
[Killer's Belt](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3968)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 150

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn belt

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Small blood-red rubies decorate this black leather belt, which is a fashion accessory for only the most bloodthirsty soldiers. When you wear this belt, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks.

**Activate—Bleeding Rubies** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You have a free hand and your last action was to deal damage to an enemy with a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) or spell attack roll; **Effect** You pull a ruby off your belt and crush it into dust. As this dust reaches the enemy you just harmed, it embeds into the skin, causing them to bleed. The target takes 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). The ruby reappears on the belt after 24 hours.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magical Medals](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3971)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Military leaders or heads of state award these special medals to commend exemplary performance by their top soldiers. They’re typically worn on a special strip of fabric near the lapel, but soldiers from different countries sometimes wear them in other places. No matter how many _magical medals_ you have, they collectively count as one invested item.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medal of Gorilla’s Might
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This steel medal resembles the head and upper body of a [gorilla](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2827). It’s typically given in recognition of physical prowess, and it grants you a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375), [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377), and [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378).

**Activate—Gorila's Second Leap** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You would fail but not critically fail an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378);  **Effect** The gorilla on the medal beats its chest, giving you another chance. You reroll the failed check.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medal of Griffon’s Heart
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This copper medal features a [griffon’s](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3034) face, wings, and talons in profile. It is given in recognition of remarkable bravery, and it grants you a +1 item bonus to saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects. While wearing this pendant, you can also cast [_forbidding ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535) as an [innate](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901) cantrip.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medal of the Wolf Pack
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

There are three [wolf’s](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3241) heads engraved on this pewter medal, typically awarded to squads who demonstrate exceptional teamwork. While wearing the _medal of the wolf pack_, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to damage rolls against enemies you are [flanking](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medal of Unicorn’s Purity
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

**Price** 1,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The profile of the [unicorn](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=504) on this silver medal has a horn that extends slightly beyond the circular border. This medal is awarded in recognition of true righteousness and exemplary service to a cause.

**Activate—Unicorn’s Grace** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You would regain Hit Points from a [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) effect;  **Effect** You regain an additional 4d6 Hit Points.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medal of Phoenix’s Fire
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

**Price** 9,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The [phoenix](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3137) on this gold medal is proud and fierce, and the medal features a border shaped like a flame. This medal is considered the highest honor in many countries and is awarded to those soldiers who have truly exemplified the ethics of their country through unbelievable service.

**Activate—Phoenix’s Sacrifice** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** Your [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition increases;  **Effect** The phoenix bursts into flames. You lose the dying condition and regain 1 Hit Point. Your [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) value does not increase. You can use this action while [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95).
name: Medal of Gorilla’s Might

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Medic's Armband](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3972)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You wear this white armband with a bright blue symbol identifying you as a combat medic. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks to change the attitudes of [diseased](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [poisoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), and [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) creatures.

**Activate—Do No Harm** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** An enemy within 30 feet targets you with a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) or a spell that deals damage;  **Effect** Your armband glows, showing that you’re here as a medic and not as a combatant. Both you and the triggering enemy take a –4 status penalty to damage rolls until the end of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medic’s Armband
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 152

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Greater Medic’s Armband
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 152

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) checks to [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2396) and [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=2399), and the status penalty to damage rolls from Do No Harm increases to –8.
name: Medic’s Armband

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Motion-Seeking Lenses](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3973)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 152

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you wear these green-tinted glasses, all the movement in the surrounding area seems to jump out at you. You gain a +1 item bonus to your [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) DC against [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405), and anyone attempting to Sneak doesn’t benefit from the circumstance bonus from [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) against your Perception DC.

**Activate—Find the Hidden** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=574), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You twist the lenses of your glasses as you look for someone hidden. You [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) with a +1 item bonus. If you find a [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) creature or object, you can [Point Out](/Actions.aspx?ID=2315) as a free action.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Motion-Seeking Lenses

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pristine Epaulets](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3975)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 152

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Gaudy and sparkling, these intricately decorated epaulets have clearly never seen a battlefield. Worn by officers with more schooling or connections than actual fighting experience, these epaulets grant you a +1 item bonus to [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) and [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) checks.

**Activate—I Meant to Say** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You critically fail a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) check;  **Effect** The _pristine epaulets_ are often worn to both tense military negotiations and social events and can help you recover from a misstep. You can reroll the check, but you must take the new result.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pristine Epaulets

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sailor's Collar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3977)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 152

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Veteran sailors like to wear this jaunty blue collar, tied with a small bow in the front and tucked into the belt. It can even save your life if you fall overboard. When wearing the sailor’s collar, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check. 

**Activate—Gasp for Air** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You fail a [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) check;  **Effect** Your collar inflates, giving you something to breathe from. You can breathe underwater for 1 minute.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shining Hackle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3978)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 152

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Some troops wear this plume of short feathers in their hats as part of a formal uniform, but the soft glow emanating from it can be useful elsewhere. While wearing the _shining hackle_, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) checks based on sight, but you also take a –1 item penalty to your [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks.

**Activate—Glowing Hackle** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640))    **Effect** Your _shining hackle_ glows even brighter, shedding [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) in a 20-foot radius (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) for the next 20 feet). This effect lasts until you [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shining Hackle

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tasset of Flexibility](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3982)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 153

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You can attach these light-brown leather flaps adorned with gold stitching to a breastplate or even clothing to protect your upper legs in battle. They give you the freedom to move your body to its limit without worrying about exposing yourself to a hit. While wearing the _tasset of flexibility_, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks.

**Activate—Lunging Attack** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The tasset helps you stretch farther than you normally could. Make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with a melee weapon, increasing your [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684) by 5 feet for that Strike.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tasset of Flexibility

This hooded cloak is lined with rough foliage, vines, and bark that never wilts or rots away. When wearing this cloak, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks when in heavily forested areas.

**Activate—One with the Woods** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For the next minute, you [ignore any difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) caused by plants and fungi, such as bushes, vines, and undergrowth.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Mask of Shifting Monstrosity](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4005)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane
](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 80

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This mask’s features shift between various nonhuman elements, with each version reveling in grotesque and monstrous variations. During the festivals in Dhuraxilis, these masks are worn by influential and rich humans (or human-appearing populations) to better blend in with the monstrous crowds as they join in the revelry. While wearing the mask, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks.

**Activate—Monstrosity Meld** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You take on the general appearance of a type of non-humanoid creature that you can see, with the transformation lasting for 8 hours. Activating this effect doesn’t change your traits or statistics, nor does it grant any of the special abilities of the creature you’re imitating. It does not change your size, so a Medium creature that assumes the shape of a manticore remains Medium sized. This effect uses the same rules as the [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) activity of [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38). Onlookers always assume you’re the chosen type of creature unless they’re actively Seeking. You gain a +4 item bonus to your Deception DC against such Perception checks and add your level even if untrained.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mask of Shifting Monstrosity

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragon Pearl](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4011)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 222

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The damage type(s) for this item must be chosen at purchase or when crafted._

**Price** 9,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When an irritant is caught within a dragon’s scales, the dragon’s body protects itself by coating the object in layers of dragon scale. Over time, it can form an item called a _dragon pearl_. Such pearls, hard and iridescent, are infused with draconic magic and can be powerful tools in the right hands. They’re usually placed in an ornate setting on a pin or necklace. Wearing the _dragon pearl_ grants you a +2 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks. 

**Activate—Pearlescent Shell** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You call protective power from the pearl to form a defensive barrier around yourself. You’re affected by an 8th-rank [_containment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1981) spell, which takes on the appearance of shimmering translucent dragon scales. The field has [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 15 to one damage type related to the dragon the pearl came from. When you activate the pearl, you choose the type from all types the dragon has immunity or resistance to plus a type based on their tradition: [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610) for [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) for [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) for [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), or [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) for [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragon Pearl

<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=143" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 229

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This palm-sized talisman hangs on a string of braided bark fibers. It resembles a leaf from some unknown tree similar to oak, carved from jade and then dipped halfway into black ink that looks wet despite feeling completely dry. The gate key was originally created to open the aiudara known as the Seventh Arch. In recent years, corrupting magic has given it additional, stranger powers. 

When you wear and invest the _Shadewither Key_, shadows gather around you. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks. However, this boon comes with great risk: any [elf](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=60) who touches the _Shadewither Key_ is immediately afflicted with the [obnubilate curse](/Curses.aspx?ID=49). 

**Activate—Umbral Double** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You touch the _Shadewither Key_ and command the shadows it casts around you to peel away. The shadows form an umbral double that resembles you in every way except for its fiery red eyes and the expression of wicked delight on its face. The double occupies the same space as you and attempts to intercept any attacks aimed at you. A creature must succeed at a DC 11 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) when targeting you with an attack, spell, or other effect; on a failure, they hit the shadowy double instead of you. The effect lasts for 1 minute or until the double is hit, whichever comes first. Once the double is hit, it’s destroyed. As long as the double exists, you don’t gain the _Shadewither Key’s_ item bonus to Stealth checks.

**Destruction** The _Shadewither Key’s_ magic can be destroyed by exposing the artifact to uncontained teleportation energies. If touched to an unlinked or malfunctioning aiudara, the _Shadewither Key_ causes the gate to connect to a new counterpart. If this happens, the key’s magic leaves it, rendering the item useless (as described in the book).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shadewither Key

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Stalk Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4024)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=113" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 230

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These black leather goggle frames have no lenses. Instead, when a character puts them on and invests them, the wearer’s eyes transform and lengthen into snail-like eyestalks. The wobbly eyestalks stretch out through the lens holes and up over the wearer’s head on lengthened optic nerves.

**Activate—Looksee** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** By focusing hard, you can watch for enemies in all directions. You gain [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) for 1 minute; during this time, you can’t be [flanked](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

[Astrolabe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4027)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 232, [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 34

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>**-Calculate** 1 minute ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Astrolabes can be used for navigation in unfamiliar or featureless locations. To use an astrolabe, the holder must be trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49). By spending 1 minute to measure the height of the stars and planets, a holder who knows the time and date can determine the latitude, and a holder who knows their latitude can determine the date and time. An astrolabe also grants a +1 item bonus to checks to identify celestial bodies. A standard astrolabe functions only on steady ground.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Energizing Pill](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4055)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 295

**Price** 180 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This special blend of potent herbs comes in the form of a compact ball the size of a marble that allows it to be held in the mouth, where it remains for 1 hour. An energizing pill has a bitter taste at first, but the longer one lets it soak, the more complex and sweet its flavor grows. It heightens your reactions, granting you a +2 item bonus to initiative rolls with Perception.

**Secondary Effect** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** You would become [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94); **Effect** Reduce the value of the condition you just gained by 1; if this reduces the value to 0, you don’t gain the triggering condition.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Energizing Pill

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Covenant Tea](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4075)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
<trait label="Tea" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=738" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 299

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This green tea is often steeped in a pot with a dried persimmon. Traditionally, all participants in a complex discussion start their conversation with a tea ceremony involving _covenant tea_, with each member of the group enjoying the covenant of the shared beverage. Some have been known to use trickery and sleight of hand to ensure that only those whose goals align with the tea preparer’s are served covenant tea, while others in the group are served non-magical (but still delicious) green tea, thus subtly tipping the balance in discussion toward those the tea server favors. This practice has resulted in some regions referring to _covenant tea_ as “trickery tea.” Regardless of what you prefer to call the tea, when you drink it, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks and to your [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) DC for 10 minutes.

---

**Activate—Tea Ceremony**10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The duration increases to 1 hour.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Covenant Tea

>
[Energizing Tea](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4076)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

---

This sweet and refreshing tea is made with a mixture of honey, lemon slices, and sliced ginger. This is considered to be the bare minimum when serving _energizing tea_, and many go above and beyond by adding additional citrus fruits or berries to the mix or refusing to serve the tea at all without also serving a full platter of spiced pastries and sweets. This golden tea has energizing properties and, when consumed, grants you a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) and [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks for 10 minutes.

---

**Activate—Tea Ceremony** 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The duration increases to 1 hour.

>
[Spiritsight Tea](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4081)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
<trait label="Tea" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=738" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 300

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Spiritsight tea_ exudes a soft blue glow, creating illumination equal to that of a candle. When consumed, your eyes take on a soft glow as well, and you can see [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) creatures and objects for 10 minutes; such creatures appear to you as translucent shapes, and they’re [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) to you. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creatures.

---

**Activate—Tea Ceremony** 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The duration of being able to see invisible creatures and objects increases to 30 minutes, and the duration of the item bonus increases to 8 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>
[Privacy Ward Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4084)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 301

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to wall

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This fulu seeks to keep thieves, spies, and unwanted attention away from a room. A depiction of a lock appears in the center of this fulu, which is in turn surrounded by circles of broken keys. When applied to a wall inside a room, all creatures within the room gain an item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks against creatures outside the room.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Limited"
>
Privacy Ward Fulu (Hallway)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 301

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The _privacy ward fulu_ can shield a room of up to 10 square feet, grants a +1 item bonus, and has a duration of 1 hour.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Limited"
>
Privacy Ward Fulu (Room)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 301

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The fulu can shield a room of up to 30 square feet, grants a +1 item bonus, and has a duration of 8 hours. If you become [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) within the room, you automatically become [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) to creatures of the fulu’s level or lower.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Limited"
>
Privacy Ward Fulu (Chamber)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 301

**Price** 120 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The fulu can shield a room of up to 90 square feet, grants a +2 item bonus, and has a duration of 24 hours. If you become hidden within the room, you automatically become undetected to creatures of the fulu’s level or lower.

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 301 **Price** 100 gp **Usage** worn cloak **Bulk** L --- While [Yaezhing](/Deities.aspx?ID=318) is feared and seldom worshipped in the open, some regions of Tian Xia see him as a god of necessary evil and their only hope for justice. This garment is often worn by bounty hunters or priests of Yaezhing, yet non-worshippers of the god of harsh justice sometimes wear this item without fear of religious persecution. The red cape appears almost black while in the shadows, with a lighter red mandala pattern on it that resembles a shuriken. When you invest a _cape of justice_, you must designate a single creature as your offender. You must have met the creature before, or the creature should have a persona well known to the public (for instance, a bandit’s name or epithet, if not their face as shown on a wanted poster). You gain a +1 item bonus to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) this creature. If you don’t designate a creature as your offender when you invest the cape of justice, or if 24 hours pass without you either slaying or capturing your offender, your thoughts become worried and distracted, and you take a –1 item penalty to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks for the next 24 hours. **Activate—Force Shuriken** ([force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** By using a free hand to grab the edge of your cape and give it a quick flourish, you cause a red, shurikenshaped bolt of force to fire at a target within 60 feet that you can see. The shuriken hits automatically and deals 1d4+1 force damage, or 2d4+2 force damage if the target is your designated offender. If the target is within 30 feet, you can attempt to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) the target as a free action after the shuriken strikes them **Activate—Enact Justice** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You produce manacles from the cape and then fling them at a Medium or Small bipedal target within 30 feet. The target must attempt a DC 18 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected, and the manacles vanish.
**Success** The manacles strike the target’s legs but fail to latch properly. The target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn, at which point the manacles vanish.
**Failure** The manacles lock on the target’s legs. The target takes a –15-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds for 1 minute, or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) or is freed by someone using [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=2412) (DC 18), after which the manacles vanish.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) for as long as the manacles remain in place.
name: Cape of Justice

The target must attempt a DC 18 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected, and the manacles vanish.
**Success** The manacles strike the target’s legs but fail to latch properly. The target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn, at which point the manacles vanish.
**Failure** The manacles lock on the target’s legs. The target takes a –15-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds for 1 minute, or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) or is freed by someone using [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=2412) (DC 18), after which the manacles vanish.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) for as long as the manacles remain in place.
name: Cape of Justice

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Fan of Falling Words](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4088)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 302

**Price** 1,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This folding handheld paper fan appears blank when first found, but if the wielder unfolds its leaves as an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action, they display an ink-brush painting of the wielder’s choice. The fan must be closed and opened again to display a different painting. While held, the fan of falling words grants you a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks to act or perform comedy, orate, or sing.

**Activate—Whispered World** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You manifest the tapestry of your words, causing the image you depict on the fan to leap off its leaves. The fan casts [_illusory scene_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570) to your specifications (DC 27 Will save). While this only takes 3 actions to [perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=2401) (as opposed to the spell’s normal 10 minutes of casting), the effect has no range, and the 50-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) it creates is centered on you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fan of Falling Words

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 302 **Price** 1,300 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- In many parts of Tian Xia, it’s tradition to mint coins with holes in the centers for the purpose of being able to thread a cord through them and tie them off for security and ease of transaction. This immaculately braided red cord is made in this tradition and can hold up to 100 coins at any given time. As long as you carry a _fortune cord_, you gain a +2 item bonus to skill checks to [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=2364). If you load up to 100 coins onto a _fortune cord_ as part of your daily preparations, you can Activate the magic item in two different ways until the next time you make your daily preparations, provided all the loaded coins are of the same type. **Activate—Merchant's Bludgeon** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** You have at least 50 coins loaded onto the _fortune cord_; **Effect** You transform the fortune cord into a _[+2 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [nunchaku](/Weapons.aspx?ID=414)_ for 1 minute. The type of coin loaded onto the cord determines the additional weapon property rune that manifests on this _+2 striking nunchaku_, as follows.
**Copper**: [_corrosive_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2834)
**Silver**: [_shock_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2847)
**Gold**: [_flaming_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838)
**Platinum**: [_frost_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2839) **Activate—Fling Coins** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Effect** You whip the _fortune cord_, causing 10 coins loaded onto it to detach and fire at a creature within 100 feet that you can see. That creature takes 4d6 damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save); the damage type depends on the type of coins you’ve loaded. Copper coins deal [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) damage, [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) coins deal electricity damage, gold coins deal [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) damage, and platinum coins deal [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) damage.
name: Fortune Cord

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Hongrui's Gratitude](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4090)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 303

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Given as thanks for honoring the memories of three unfortunate travelers who met a tragic end in the Willowshore Hinterlands, this red-hooded cloak is decorated with an image of an elaborately dressed performer whose two companions hold a lantern and umbrella for her.

**Activate—Release Lantern** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You don’t have the Release Umbrella activation of this cloak active; **Effect** With a wave of the cloak to the left, you cause the image of the lantern-holding companion on the cloak to vanish. A floating red lantern appears at your side and follows you at an arm’s length while casting bright light in a 20-foot radius (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) for the next 20 feet) like a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2760). For 1 hour, while the light from this lantern is active, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks. You can Release Lantern again to [dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the effect.

**Activate—Release Umbrella** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You don’t have the Release Lantern activation of this cloak active; **Effect** With a wave of the cloak to the right, you cause the image of the umbrella-holding companion on the cloak to vanish. An indestructible red wax paper umbrella appears above you and follows you, shielding you from weather effects, such as rain or bright sunlight. For 1 hour, while this umbrella is active, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks. You can Release Umbrella again to [dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hongrui's Gratitude

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 303 **Price** 1,300 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 1 --- This hourglass-shaped drum has two heads, each of which produces a different sound when struck. When played together, the resonance of the two sounds is effective in repelling evil spirits. This drum can be played as an instrument, granting a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45). **Activate—Memories of Life** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You play both heads of the drums, generating a pulse of energy that targets one creature within 30 feet that has the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) trait. This deals 5d8 [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) damage (DC 28 Fortitude save) to that creature; if the undead also has the [ghost](/Traits.aspx?ID=753) trait, the ghost becomes [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 if it fails the save. **Activate—Memories of Joy** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You raucously play both heads of the drums, accompanied with shouting and dancing. This display generates a pulse of energy in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) from you. All [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creatures within this area must attempt a DC 28 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature takes no damage.
**Success** The creature takes 2d8 vitality damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 5d8 vitality damage; if the creature has the [ghost](/Traits.aspx?ID=753) trait, it becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 10d8 vitality damage; if the creature has the ghost trait, it becomes stupefied 2 for 1 minute.
name: Lacquered Waist Drum

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 304 **Price** 1,820 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 **Base Weapon** [Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367) --- This ash wood staff is topped by a long tassel of bleachedwhite horsehair. Historically carried by the most important of court officials throughout various regions and periods of Tian Xia’s history, purgatory emissary’s staves are also strongly associated with [psychopomps](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=458) serving punitive sentences. It’s thought this connection is a comical nod to the bureaucratic nature of the afterlife. Psychopomps and spirits tend to regard you with a level of respect while you carry a purgatory emissary’s staff. While you wield this staff, you receive a +2 item bonus to all skill checks to adjust a psychopomp’s or spirit’s attitude. When used as a weapon, a purgatory emissary’s staff is a _[+2 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [ghost touch ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367)_. **Activate—Cast a Spell** ; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list.
  • **Cantrip** [_vitality lash_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1744)
  • **1st** [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1660)
  • **2nd** [_clear mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469), [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663)
  • **3rd** [_holy light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557), [_ring of truth_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1656)
  • **4th** [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), t[_alking corpse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712)
  • **5th** [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)
name: Purgatory Emissary's Staff

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Spider Lily Tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4098)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 305

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The spider lily tattoo marks you as a trusted member of Granny Hu’s network. This crimson tattoo fades and becomes invisible within a day of being applied, reappearing only when you Activate it, when you gain the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) condition, or when you die. The higher the doomed value, the more vivid the color. [Additional rules for magical tattoos appear here](/Rules.aspx?ID=1495). 

If your tattoo is plainly visible, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks against all creatures that can see the tattoo, but you take a –1 item penalty to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) on those who understand the actual meaning of the _spider lily tattoo_ (including all of Willowshore’s citizens).

**Activate—Reveal Tattoo** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))   **Requirements** Your tattoo isn't currently visible; **Effect** You make the tattoo visible. You can use this activation again to make the tattoo not visible, unless you have the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) condition.

**Activate—Dead Like You** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** An [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creature detects you for the first time, and you’re aware of the undead creature;  **Effect** The _spider lily tattoo_ manipulates your life force to make you appear to be [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) for a short time. Attempt a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check against the triggering undead creature’s Perception DC. On a success, the triggering undead believes you’re undead as well—a mindless undead is likely to ignore you, while a sapient undead might react with curiosity or confusion. You can continue attempting Deception checks each round as a single action to [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the effect for up to 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spider Lily Tattoo

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Injigo's Loving Embrace](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4099)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 305

**Price** 675 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This net was woven by the [jorogumo](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1200) Injigo, who presented it as a gift to her [gnome](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=61) sweetheart, Mara, to keep her safe in their mountain cottage whenever Injigo left home to go on hunting expeditions. 

_Injigo’s Loving Embrace_ functions as a typical [net](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2738). You gain a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) with the net.

**Activate—Ensnare Ghost** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You spin the net in the direction of an [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creature within 30 feet that you can see. Lines of silken force whip out to lash around the incorporeal creature, who must succeed at a DC 25 Reflex save or become [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) until the end of your next turn. You can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this activation for up to 1 minute, and the creature must succeed at a DC 25 check to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) the net.

**Activate—A Night of Melancholic Dreams** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** You wrap the net around you like a blanket as you prepare to sleep. As long as you get 8 hours of sleep or analogous rest, you dream of writing love letters to someone you adore: this could be an actual paramour, someone you’re interested in, or someone unknown whose identity you don’t recall after waking. When you wake, you’re filled with a sense of melancholia about those unsent letters, but these feelings distract you from other, more unpleasant thoughts, granting you [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) resistance 5 and a +1 item bonus to saving throws against mental effects for the next 8 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Injigo's Loving Embrace

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Kinburi's Sandals of Bounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4100)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 306

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These elevated wooden sandals once belonged to a haughty [kitsune](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=38) named Kinburi. It was well known that her swiftness and grace were unmatched, and observers described her as seeming to glide above the earth with each step. She often challenged others to wager on her speed, but as people began to grow wise, she was forced to conceal her identity or promise to forfeit her sandals if she lost.

These sandals give you a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) and [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378). In addition, when you use the [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) action, you can move 5 feet further if jumping horizontally or 3 feet higher if jumping vertically.

**Activate—Light-Footed Bound** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You loudly boast about your grace and agility, then take any combination of two [High Jumps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) or [Long Jumps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378). You don’t need to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) first, and between the two jumps, you need only to come into contact with a physical object before making the second jump; this object doesn’t need to be one that can bear your weight, or even be attached to the ground—you can jump off of a floating feather or a thin branch or even off a paper wall as needed. The sandals’ item bonus increases to +2 for these jumps.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Kinburi's Sandals of Bounding

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Ghost Scarf](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4104)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 307

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn belt or scarf

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This 6-foot-long scarf shimmers with the silvery light of the River of Souls while worn, providing illumination equivalent to that of a candle. You can deactivate or activate this radiance as an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action. The dangling lengths of a _ghost scarf_ softly billow in the presence of haunts, granting the wearer a +1 item bonus to all [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks and Perception DCs to resolve discovering a [haunt](/Traits.aspx?ID=622) or rolling initiative when a haunt triggers.

**Activate—Ghost Slayer's Caress** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The scarf extends silvery threads that wrap around a weapon you carry, granting the effects of a _[ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840)_ property rune to that weapon for 5 minutes. If the weapon already bears a _ghost touch_ rune, you instead gain a +1 item bonus to Fortitude saves against effects from [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) for 5 minutes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ghost Scarf

    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Hairpin of Blooming Flowers](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4105)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 307

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The flower that adorns this hairpin is a blooming lotus flower that varies in color; it regrows in a day after the item is activated. Whether or not the hairpin’s flower is in bloom, as long as you wear it and it’s invested, it grants a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks.

**Activate—Scatter Petals** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You pluck the flower from the hairpin and scatter the petals, creating a flurry of color in a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) centered on you. You become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) for 1 minute or until you move from your current location. Any creature within the 10-foot burst when you Activate the Item must succeed at a DC 23 Reflex save or become [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until the end of its next turn (or [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) until the end of its next turn on a critical failure). The flower blooms again the next day.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hairpin of Blooming Flowers

    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Resolute Mind Wrap](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4107)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The followers of the Tan Sugi monastery understood the value of protecting the mind from the intrusion of violence and anger and infused this strip of striped green and brown cloth with their beliefs. Wrapped around the head, the stripes almost evoke the patterns of sugi branches coiled around the wearer’s skull. The resolute mind wrap grants you mental [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5.

**Activate—Shield Thoughts** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You attempt a Will saving throw against a mental effect;  **Effect** The resolute mind wrap clings more tightly to your head, granting you a +1 item bonus to your Will saving throw. If you succeed at this saving throw, the resistance to mental damage granted by the _resolute mind wrap_ increases to 10 for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hoax-Hunter's Kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4115)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 63

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wooden box unfolds into a stack of hinged trays holding calipers, magnifying lenses, acids and reagents, comparison sketches of species commonly mistaken for one another, and glass slides for specimen testing. When you use this kit to study accounts of a creature or what it left behind (such as spoor, tracks, or fur samples), you gain a +1 item bonus to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about the creature or to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) the creature. In addition, if you fail to Recall Knowledge about the creature (but don't critically fail), you're able to eliminate at least one possibility of a common type of animal. For instance, you might be able to verify the creature isn't an [ankhrav](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=361), even if you get no further information.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hoax-Hunter's Kit

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Conrasu Coin](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4138)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=709" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 155

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These wooden discs sometimes form spontaneously at the Wood Pile, a secret conrasu location suffused with extraplanar energy. A _conrasu coin_ can be activated to call upon the power of a specific type of aeon.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Conrasu Coin (Arbiter)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 155

**Price** 7 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

**Trigger** You attempt a Diplomacy check to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392).

 This coin has small wings like an arbiter, the messenger and diplomat aeon. When you activate the coin, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus on the Diplomacy check, which increases to a +3 circumstance bonus to Make an  Impression on a creature with the [aeon](/Traits.aspx?ID=525) trait.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Conrasu Coin (Bythos)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 155

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

**Trigger** You're targeted with an attack but the opponent hasn't rolled yet.

 This cog-like coin bears an hourglass on one side and the four-armed silhouette of a bythos—aeons that steward over time—on the other. When activated, you catch a glimpse of your body moving on one of its potential timelines in response to danger and you follow those movements to dodge. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the attack, and after resolving the attack, you can Stride up to 10 feet without triggering reactions. If you have the [Nimble Roll](/Feats.aspx?ID=4953) feat and the attack misses, you can Stride up to 20 feet instead.

<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=567" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 161

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Smiling faces are inscribed about this silver band. You become a fluent weaver of fictions, gaining a +2 item bonus to Deception checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389), Deception DCs against [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302), and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks for storytelling. Whenever you're under suspicion or being questioned by an authority figure, you find yourself compulsively spinning absurd, tall tales that are so unconvincing that they make you look guilty even when you're innocent. The ring's bonuses vanish, and any listener can quickly determine you're lying. Nevertheless, you're completely unable to be honest in such situations.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Taleteller's Ring

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Self-Emptying Pocket](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4155)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Contract" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=329" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 165

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You never perform to an empty crowd after promising any profits to a phantom in a playhouse, sealed by holes that appear in your pockets. By meeting with the attendees at your events, you can use Performance instead of Diplomacy to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391). When you would use Performance to [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=2364), you don't earn any gold pieces, as the money disappears before you can even count it.

 The entity that holds your _bargained contract_ can influence your reception any time you perform for a crowd or Activate the pocket. If they influence your performance favorably, you gain a +2 item bonus to your Performance check. If they influence your performance unfavorably, you take a -2 item penalty to your Performance check. If you've failed to earn the entity money in quite a while, it often influences your performance unfavorably. 

**Activate—Beguiling Presence** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You exercise all of your charm on a creature, turning a chance meeting into an impromptu performance that commands attention. Attempt a single [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check against the Perception DC of the creature. On a success, the creature is affected as though by a successful Deception check to [Create a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=2387). The entity that holds your contract can influence this performance as well.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Self-Emptying Pocket

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Ring of Fair Assessment](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4182)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hellbreakers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 212

**Price** 35 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While the Kalistocrats of Druma profiteer off selling weapons to both Hellbreakers and Hellknights, they also offer options suitable for intelligence and counterintelligence efforts. This white gold ring grants you a +1 item bonus to skill checks to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2363) that's primarily numerical or mathematical in nature as well as to skill checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) and [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394) to convince others of the fairness or accuracy of your judgments.

**Activate—Fair Assessment** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The Prophecies of Kalistrade reveal the secrets of wealth to you; this appears as a balance sheet of numbers only you can see. Choose an area of up to 1 cubic foot within 30 feet; you can estimate the value in gold pieces of any items within this area. If you attempt to estimate the value of items worn or held by a creature, you must succeed at a Society check to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2363) against their Will DC.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ring of Fair Assessment

This bracelet has three charms depicting a dagger, a shield, and a rose. While wearing this bracelet, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) checks. Whenever you roll a success to free someone from manacles, it counts as two successes (three on a critical success).

**Activate—No Chains** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A creature attempts to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) you; **Requirements** You are aware of the creature and aren't [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58); **Effect** The bracelet yanks your body in one direction, attempting to throw off your attacker's grip. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude DC against the triggering Grapple.
