<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spirit-Singer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4242)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 83

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Etheric spirit-singers create an eerie spectral sound based on the presence of spiritual essence and other ethereal energies. All spirit-singers can be used as musical instruments by manipulating the sensitivity to ethereal energies. However, they have an even greater benefit in areas heavy with spiritual essence. When played in the presence of a significant spiritual disturbance, such as a haunt or an incorporeal undead, a spirit‑singer grants you a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. While playing a spirit-singer, you also gain a +1 item bonus to checks to detect a [haunt](/Traits.aspx?ID=622) or [incorporeal ](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722), and you can roll a check to notice a haunt even if you aren’t actively Searching for it, due to the distortions of the spirit-singer’s music. A haunt or incorporeal undead that is intelligent enough to notice the effects it is having on the spirit-singer’s music and that can’t otherwise communicate with the living might choose to use the spirit-singer to do so if it wishes. For instance, it could try to guide the spirit-singer player towards a location by creating distortions in that direction or, if it understands language, it could try to answer questions by creating one distortion for yes and two distortions for no. Unless stated otherwise in its usage entry, a spirit-singer functions like a heavy musical instrument; rather than carrying it, the musician places the spirit‑singer in a particular position and uses both hands to play.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spirit-Singer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4242)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 83

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Etheric spirit-singers create an eerie spectral sound based on the presence of spiritual essence and other ethereal energies. All spirit-singers can be used as musical instruments by manipulating the sensitivity to ethereal energies. However, they have an even greater benefit in areas heavy with spiritual essence. When played in the presence of a significant spiritual disturbance, such as a haunt or an incorporeal undead, a spirit‑singer grants you a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. While playing a spirit-singer, you also gain a +1 item bonus to checks to detect a [haunt](/Traits.aspx?ID=622) or [incorporeal ](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722), and you can roll a check to notice a haunt even if you aren’t actively Searching for it, due to the distortions of the spirit-singer’s music. A haunt or incorporeal undead that is intelligent enough to notice the effects it is having on the spirit-singer’s music and that can’t otherwise communicate with the living might choose to use the spirit-singer to do so if it wishes. For instance, it could try to guide the spirit-singer player towards a location by creating distortions in that direction or, if it understands language, it could try to answer questions by creating one distortion for yes and two distortions for no. Unless stated otherwise in its usage entry, a spirit-singer functions like a heavy musical instrument; rather than carrying it, the musician places the spirit‑singer in a particular position and uses both hands to play.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spirit-Singer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4242)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 83

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Etheric spirit-singers create an eerie spectral sound based on the presence of spiritual essence and other ethereal energies. All spirit-singers can be used as musical instruments by manipulating the sensitivity to ethereal energies. However, they have an even greater benefit in areas heavy with spiritual essence. When played in the presence of a significant spiritual disturbance, such as a haunt or an incorporeal undead, a spirit‑singer grants you a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. While playing a spirit-singer, you also gain a +1 item bonus to checks to detect a [haunt](/Traits.aspx?ID=622) or [incorporeal ](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722), and you can roll a check to notice a haunt even if you aren’t actively Searching for it, due to the distortions of the spirit-singer’s music. A haunt or incorporeal undead that is intelligent enough to notice the effects it is having on the spirit-singer’s music and that can’t otherwise communicate with the living might choose to use the spirit-singer to do so if it wishes. For instance, it could try to guide the spirit-singer player towards a location by creating distortions in that direction or, if it understands language, it could try to answer questions by creating one distortion for yes and two distortions for no. Unless stated otherwise in its usage entry, a spirit-singer functions like a heavy musical instrument; rather than carrying it, the musician places the spirit‑singer in a particular position and uses both hands to play.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Violet Ray](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4243)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 83

**Price** 1,893 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

One of the many wondrous devices brought into the world by Stasian technology is the seemingly miraculous violet ray. Physicians claim it anything from headaches to heartburn, or nausea to deafness, all with an easy and painless treatment. The device is a glass vacuum with an insulated handle connected to a small Stasian coil. When powered, the glass tube fills with purple light and becomes warm to the touch. Pressing the tube to one’s body is said to increase blood flow, eliminate toxins, and many other beneficial effects. A violet ray functions as a set of [healer’s tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2727) and provides a +2 item bonus to [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) checks to [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2396), [Treat Disease](/Actions.aspx?ID=2397), [Treat Poison](/Actions.aspx?ID=2398), or [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=2399).

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You apply the violet ray to an adjacent creature and attempt to counteract the [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59), [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition with a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) level of 6 and a counteract modifier of +22, using the source of the condition to determine the condition’s counteract level and DC. If the condition was caused by an ongoing effect and you don’t remove that effect, the condition returns after 1 minute. Each use of this ability can only counteract a single condition.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Violet Ray

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Aromatic Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4292)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 168

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The components of this ammunition emit a strong smell when combined during its activation. A creature hit by an activated aromatic ammunition gains a distinct odor for up to 1 hour or until the scent is washed off (requiring at least a gallon of water and 10 minutes of scrubbing). Any creatures within 30 feet smell the target, allowing even those with a weak sense of smell to detect its presence, and all creatures gain a +1 item bonus to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) the affected creature for as long as it has the odor. A creature that has imprecise or precise scent doubles the range at which they can detect the target using their scent compared to the normal range of their scent.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Aromatic Ammunition

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Camouflage Suit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4305)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 178

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This lightweight mesh fits easily over light armor. The suit is designed to incorporate local flora and ground clutter into the mesh to help you blend in seamlessly with the environment. Due to the abrasive nature of the materials used, this item is unsuitable for use by unarmored characters. You can prepare the suit for use within your current environment by using an exploration activity that takes at least 10 minutes, but sometimes longer if the materials are hard to find or the environment is unusual enough to warrant additional difficulty in preparing camouflage that can blend with it consistently.

A suit prepared in this manner grants you a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks while you attempt to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) in the specific environment it has been prepared for The suit remains usable in this manner until you rest for the night, though it doesn’t grant the benefit whenever you aren’t in the appropriate environment. The GM might rule that some environments are unusual enough that you can’t create a camouflage suit appropriate for the environment. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>
Camouflage Suit
</title>

</traits>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Darkvision Scope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4316)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 180

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm or crossbow (scope)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These scopes, popular with snipers and other sneaky sharpshooters who ply their trade in the dead of night, incorporate clouded crystals with magical properties into their design. While relatively useless under normal lighting conditions, these crystals can help bring things into focus when used in dim light. The scope is then given an enchantment to enhance these properties for use in darkness. The scope grants you a +1 item bonus to Perception checks involving sight in areas of [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) visible through the scope (as well as in areas of [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2404), if the scope has been activated).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You gain [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) until the beginning of your next turn, as long as you continue to look through the scope.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Darkvision Scope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4316)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 180

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm or crossbow (scope)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These scopes, popular with snipers and other sneaky sharpshooters who ply their trade in the dead of night, incorporate clouded crystals with magical properties into their design. While relatively useless under normal lighting conditions, these crystals can help bring things into focus when used in dim light. The scope is then given an enchantment to enhance these properties for use in darkness. The scope grants you a +1 item bonus to Perception checks involving sight in areas of [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) visible through the scope (as well as in areas of [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2404), if the scope has been activated).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You gain [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) until the beginning of your next turn, as long as you continue to look through the scope.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2 and activating the scope grants [greater darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2410) until the beginning of your next turn, as long as you continue to look through the scope.
name: Darkvision Scope (Greater)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magnetite Scope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4317)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 181

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm (scope)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

More of a field projector than a scope, this small cylinder of magnetite has been enchanted to spread and accelerate the shrapnel of a scatter weapon further than the weapon’s natural capabilities. Furthermore, some of the floating magnetite inside the scope’s structure can help you when attempting to determine the your allies’ locations. The magnetite scope grants you a +2 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=2406) when using the scope to assist you in navigating. This scope can only be attached to firearms with the scatter trait.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** The next Strike you make this round with the gun to which the scope is attached has its scatter radius increased by 5 feet.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 800 gp

</row>

</column>

>

</title>

</traits>

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +3, and activating the scope increases the scatter radius by 10 feet.

>
[Magnifying Scope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4318)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 181

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm or crossbow (scope)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These scopes use magically enhanced lenses to extend the range of your weapon and help spot distant foes. The scope grants you a +1 item bonus to visual Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) creatures through the scope.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** While gazing through the scope, you zoom in on your targets to make it easier to hit them at a distance. You increase the [range](/Rules.aspx?ID=2195) increment of the weapon to which the scope is attached by 5 feet until the beginning of your next turn or until you’re no longer wielding the weapon, whichever comes first.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scope of Truth](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4320)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 182

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons. Replace the word "transmutation" in the scope of truth with the word "polymorph."_

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm or crossbow (scope)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The _scope of truth_ is a bit bigger than most to accommodate larger lenses, which have been magically prepared with tiny shards from crystal balls to reveal the truth. The scope grants you a +2 item bonus to Perception checks made to Seek in areas you can see through the scope.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For the next 10 minutes, you can see things through the scope as they actually are. The GM rolls a secret [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check with a +20 counteract modifier and a counteract level of 7 against any [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629) or [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157) in the area, but only for the purpose of determining whether you see through it, not to end the spell or effect. For instance, if the check succeeds against a [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) spell, you can see the creature’s true form, but you don’t end the spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Scope of Truth

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bastion of the Inheritor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4374)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 14

**Price** 1,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 5
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Bastion Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=17)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Worn by [Iomedae’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=285) prestigious knights, this _[+2 resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [bastion plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=17)_ is emblazoned with the Inheritor’s religious symbol and sports a white cloak. While the cloak is white, this armor grants you a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392), provided your target has no enmity toward Iomedae.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;     **Effect** The armor’s cloak becomes red for 1 minute. As long as the cloak is red, you gain the benefits of the armor’s deflect melee trait without needing to spend an additional action to activate it during each turn.

**Craft Requirements** You worship Iomedae.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bastion of the Inheritor

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You’re in a body of water; **Effect** For 10 minutes, you don’t sink if you haven’t succeeded at a [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) action on a turn, and if you’re submerged, you automatically ascend 10 feet at the end of your turn.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Auric Noodles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4442)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 47

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Auric noodles are boiled, then pan-fried and tossed with sliced vegetables and a sticky, savory sauce incorporating alchemical reagents. After you eat the noodles, they boost your ability to sense magic for 24 hours or until you make your next daily preparations, whichever comes first. You gain a +1 item bonus to checks to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2365), and you can move at full speed while using the [Detect Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2624) exploration activity.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Auric Noodles

After you eat the noodles, they boost your ability to sense magic for 24 hours or until you make your next daily preparations, whichever comes first. You gain a +1 item bonus to checks to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2365), and you can move at full speed while using the [Detect Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2624) exploration activity.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cinnamon Seers](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4443)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 47

**Price** 14 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Zippy, alchemically treated cinnamon suffuses cinnamon seers, a rock candy with a lively taste that provides a mental boost. A cinnamon seer remains in your mouth for 1 hour, its stimulating flavor granting you a +1 item bonus to checks to Recall Knowledge. 

**Secondary Effect** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Trigger** You gain no information from a [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) check; **Effect** Reroll the triggering check. If this was a secret check, the GM rerolls rather than you; the candy doesn’t give you any insight into what the GM rolled, so in that case, you’re rerolling based only on guesswork. The seer becomes inert. You become temporarily immune to cinnamon seers until the next time you make your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

</row>

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

---

If fried, a [bulette’s](/Monsters.aspx?ID=61) thick forefoot scales render into crunchy snacks. Alchemists add reagents to the frying oil to enhance the scales’ properties and flavor. For 1 minute after eating a delve scale, you gain a burrow Speed of 15 feet and a +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378).

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Diplomat's Charcuterie](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4447)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 47

**Price** 9 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>10 minutes (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A common sight at political gatherings, a diplomat’s charcuterie has fine meats, cheeses, nuts, fruits, and other finger foods mixed with reagents to engender friendly feelings between those consuming them. Contents of the plate vary by chef and the intended palates, from the hearty sausages and hard cheese of charcuterie from the Lands of the Linnorm Kings to the hot-pepper cheese curds and smoked almonds of Thuvian platters. After Activating the charcuterie by eating it, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) and [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302). These bonuses last 24 hours or until you make your next [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), whichever comes first.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Balance, Maneuver in Flight, and Squeeze when the Chocolate syrup is selected
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 48 **Price** 27 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- Containing neither eggs nor cream, an egg cream fizz contains milk or nut milk, sparkling water, and flavored syrup, frothed and chilled. Upon drinking, you feel lighter and more buoyant, gaining a +5-foot item bonus to your Speed for 10 minutes. During this time, you also gain another effect determined by the drink’s syrup, which is chosen when the drink is created.
  • **Chocolate** You gain a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369), [Maneuver in Flight](/Actions.aspx?ID=2371), and [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372).
  • **Strawberry** You gain a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298), and [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381).
  • **Vanilla** You can [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) into [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366).
name: Egg Cream Fizz

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 49 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- A popular choice for [investigators](/Classes.aspx?ID=59) studying alchemy, insight coffee is infused with alchemical flavoring during percolation. For 1 hour after you drink an insight coffee, you use d8s instead of d6s for your extra damage from the strategic strike class feature, if you have it. You also gain an item bonus to checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) with a skill determined by the blend chosen when the item is crafted.
  • **Double Coffee** [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)
  • **Hazelnut** [ Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)
  • **Mocha** [ Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)
  • **Vanilla** [ Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)
  • **Pumpkin Spice** [ Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)
  • **Toffee** [ Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42)
Insight Coffee (Lesser) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 49 **Price** 40 gp --- The item bonus is +2. Insight Coffee (Moderate) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 49 **Price** 375 gp --- The item bonus is +3. Insight Coffee (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 49 **Price** 7,500 gp --- The item bonus is +4.
name: Insight Coffee (Lesser)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Journeybread](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4454)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 49

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Journeybread contains a mix of fruits, nuts, and grains with an alchemical boost. Eating one journeybread provides all the food and water you need for a day. If you [subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368) on nothing else for a week, you become temporarily immune to journeybread until you eat real food and drink water normally for 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Journeybread
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 49

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Journeybread (Power)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 49

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

In addition to the benefits of journeybread, power journeybread grants you a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375), and [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381), and to Fortitude saving throws against being [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91). The number of minutes you can Hustle changes to your Constitution modifier × 20. These benefits last for 4 hours.
name: Journeybread (Power)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mender's Soup](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4455)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 49

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>10 minutes (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This hot, broth-based soup typically contains tubers, leeks, zesty spices, reagents, and if desired, the meat of livestock. Civic authorities commission batches of mender’s soup for workers if a tricky job is on the agenda. After you eat the soup, its effects last 24 hours or until you make your next [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), whichever comes first. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks to [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=2384) and restore an additional 5 Hit Points to items you successfully Repair during this time. 

If you eat mender’s soup over the entire period required to attempt a Crafting check to Craft (typically a minimum of eating the soup each day for 4 days), the +1 bonus from the soup can be applied to that check, too.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mender's Soup

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Phantom Roll](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4458)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 50

**Price** 13 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>10 minutes (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A phantom roll contains vegetables, greens, and fine, clear noodles, all wrapped in transparent, edible starch paper and alchemically treated and laced with a tangy sauce. Upon eating the roll, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks you attempt during the [Avoid Notice](/Actions.aspx?ID=2622) exploration activity. You can also Avoid Notice at full Speed or combine it with [Investigate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2627) or Scout while moving at half Speed. These effects expire 24 hours after you eat the roll or when you make your next [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), whichever comes first.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Phantom Roll

<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />

</row>

---

A sparkling candy coating covers a sprite apple. For 10 minutes after consuming a sprite apple, you shed [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) for the next 20 feet). While shedding this light, you can’t be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) if you’re visible, and if you’re [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83), you’re concealed instead rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96). The light matches the vibrant color of the apple’s candy coating. Creatures in the bright light are subject to another effect, depending on the type of apple.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tracker's Stew](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4466)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 51

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>10 minutes (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Alchemical reagents add punch to tracker’s stew: a rich, fiery mixture of tomatoes, ground nuts, onions, and tubers, often with poultry added. It’s usually served with or over rice or noodles. Once you’ve eaten the stew, it improves your ability to [sense](/Actions.aspx?ID=2406) and follow tracks for 24 hours or until you make your next daily preparations, whichever comes first. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to [Cover Tracks](/Actions.aspx?ID=2407) and [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408). You can do either while moving at full speed or both while moving at half speed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tracker's Stew

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Choker-Arm Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4491)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your limbs become extremely limber, letting you stretch and twist to extreme degrees at the cost of fine motor skills. 

**Benefit** You gain the listed item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296), [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372), and [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370), and you increase your reach by the listed amount. 

**Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) checks, and attack rolls, and a –1 penalty per damage die to all weapon and unarmed attack damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Choker-Arm Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +1, your reach increases by 5 feet, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Choker-Arm Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +2, your reach increases by 5 feet, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Choker-Arm Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +3, your reach increases by 10 feet, and the duration is 1 hour.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Choker-Arm Mutagen (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +4, your reach increases by 15 feet, and the duration is 1 hour.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Choker-Arm Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4491)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your limbs become extremely limber, letting you stretch and twist to extreme degrees at the cost of fine motor skills. 

**Benefit** You gain the listed item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296), [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372), and [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370), and you increase your reach by the listed amount. 

**Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) checks, and attack rolls, and a –1 penalty per damage die to all weapon and unarmed attack damage.

</column>

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Shove and Trip
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Deadweight Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4494)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your joints loosen and bones thicken, making your body incredibly weighty and difficult to maneuver around. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) and [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382), to your Fortitude and Reflex DCs against attempts to Shove or Trip you, and to saving throws against effects that attempt to force you to move or knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

**Drawback** You gain the [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=70) condition and can’t remove it while under the effects of the mutagen.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Deadweight Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +1, and the duration is 1 minute.

</traits>

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +2, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Deadweight Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +3, and the duration is 1 hour. You can attempt to Shove or Trip creatures up to two sizes larger than you.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Deadweight Mutagen (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +4, and the duration is 1 hour. You can attempt to Shove or Trip creatures up to three sizes larger than you.

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spiderfoot Brew](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4499)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=113" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 61

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sticky fluid is made from the silk glands of [giant spiders](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=474). When you drink a spiderfoot brew, tiny hairs grow on your hands and feet, granting you a [climb Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2349) and an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks made to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) for the listed duration.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Theatrical Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4501)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 61

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Developed and widely used by students at the Kitharodian Academy in Oppara, the theatrical mutagen stimulates the creative centers of your brain. This causes your movements to become exaggerated and your voice to become clear. However, the erratic surges of inspiration overload your senses, making it difficult to focus on mundane tasks.

**Benefit** You gain the listed item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks, and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. If you’re untrained in any of these skills, your proficiency bonus is equal to your level instead of +0. You also gain the listed status bonus to your Speed.

**Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks and Will saves. After any round where you don’t spend at least 1 action to [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) with an object, [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=2401), [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304), or [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), you’re [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Theatrical Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 61

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to rolls is +1, the bonus to Speed is +5 feet, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Theatrical Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 61

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to rolls is +2, the bonus to Speed is +5 feet, and the duration is 1 minute.

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

</row>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Theatrical Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4501)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 61

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Developed and widely used by students at the Kitharodian Academy in Oppara, the theatrical mutagen stimulates the creative centers of your brain. This causes your movements to become exaggerated and your voice to become clear. However, the erratic surges of inspiration overload your senses, making it difficult to focus on mundane tasks.

**Benefit** You gain the listed item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks, and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. If you’re untrained in any of these skills, your proficiency bonus is equal to your level instead of +0. You also gain the listed status bonus to your Speed.

**Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks and Will saves. After any round where you don’t spend at least 1 action to [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) with an object, [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=2401), [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304), or [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), you’re [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</row>

</column>

---

Developed and widely used by students at the Kitharodian Academy in Oppara, the theatrical mutagen stimulates the creative centers of your brain. This causes your movements to become exaggerated and your voice to become clear. However, the erratic surges of inspiration overload your senses, making it difficult to focus on mundane tasks.

**Benefit** You gain the listed item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks, and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. If you’re untrained in any of these skills, your proficiency bonus is equal to your level instead of +0. You also gain the listed status bonus to your Speed.

**Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks and Will saves. After any round where you don’t spend at least 1 action to [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) with an object, [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=2401), [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304), or [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), you’re [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn.

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Intimidation checks based on the Dread Ampoule's item bonus
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dread Helm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4509)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Fear" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=68" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 63

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The faceplate of a dread helm has a fierce visage that magnifies the effects of fear. Cleverly concealed within is a reservoir that can hold a single [dread ampoule](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3292), which takes 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions to install. A loaded dread helm can be activated to atomize the dread ampoule, creating a fear‑inducing mist that hangs around your face. The mist grants an item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks equal to the dread ampoule’s item bonus. The mist also deals mental damage equal to the dread ampoule’s splash damage to all creatures within a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) other than you. The effects last for 3 rounds, consuming the dread ampoule, and once the atomization process has begun it can’t be deactivated.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dread Helm

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Living Leaf Weave](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4511)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 64

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leaf Weave](/Armor.aspx?ID=28)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This suit of [leaf weave armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=28) is specially modified to metabolize the alchemical accelerants in medicinal compounds. A special receptacle in the armor can hold an [elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3308), which takes 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions to install. A loaded suit of living leaf weave can be activated to grow slithering vines from the armor, granting an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to Grapple, to your Fortitude DC to resist [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376), [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383), or [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) attempts, and to your Reflex DCs to resist Trip attempts. The effects last for 3 rounds, consuming the elixir of life, and once the reaction has begun it can’t be deactivated.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Living Leaf Weave

<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 64

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leaf Weave](/Armor.aspx?ID=28)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This suit of [leaf weave armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=28) is specially modified to metabolize the alchemical accelerants in medicinal compounds. A special receptacle in the armor can hold an [elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3308), which takes 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions to install. A loaded suit of living leaf weave can be activated to grow slithering vines from the armor, granting an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to Grapple, to your Fortitude DC to resist [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376), [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383), or [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) attempts, and to your Reflex DCs to resist Trip attempts. The effects last for 3 rounds, consuming the elixir of life, and once the reaction has begun it can’t be deactivated.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magnetic Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4512)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 64

**Price** 130 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=19)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Copper rings spiral around this [steel shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=19). Twin electrical probes near the grip can socket into a jar of moderate (or higher leveled) [bottled lightning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3290), which takes 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions to install. A loaded magnetic shield can be activated to convert it into an electromagnet. When an activated magnetic shield is raised, the circumstance bonus increases by 1 against attacks made with weapons primarily made of metal. If you use a [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) against a creature attacking you with such a weapon, you also gain a +1 item bonus to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) attempts against that weapon until the end of your next turn. The shield remains charged for 3 rounds, consuming the bottled lightning, and once the reaction has begun it can’t be deactivated.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ooze Skin](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4513)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 64

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41) has been treated with extract from [oozes](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=453), which can be reactivated in the presence of a strong acid. A receptacle in the armor can hold an [acid flask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3286), which takes 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions to install. A loaded suit of ooze skin can be activated to cause the leather to weep slippery protoplasm, granting an item bonus to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) and [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372) checks equal to the acid flask’s item bonus. The protoplasm also irritates the skin on prolonged contact, causing any creature that [grapples](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) or [swallows](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77) you to take acid damage equal to the acid flask’s splash damage. Ooze skin remains activated for a number of rounds equal to the level of the acid flask installed. The effects consume the acid flask, and once the reaction has begun it can’t be deactivated.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ooze Skin

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Powered Full Plate](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4515)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 65

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 4
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Stasian actuators help the limbs of this [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50) move of their own accord, as long as they’re supplied with power. A chamber in the chest plate can hold a single [bottled lightning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3290), which takes 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions to install. A loaded suit of powered full plate can be activated to lower the armor’s Strength requirement by 1 plus the item bonus of the loaded bomb. The armor’s normal penalties still apply, based on this altered Strength. However, add the bottled lightning’s item bonus to your [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375), [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377), [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378), and [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380). These effects last for 10 minutes. The armor uses up the bottled lightning during the duration and can’t be activated again until a new one is installed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Powered Full Plate

When you drink sweet, sky-blue _Elysian dew_, for 1 minute, you gain a 10-foot aura that evokes the vitality of [Elysium](/Planes.aspx?ID=40), causing nearby objects to seem more colorful and plants to stand taller. You and any ally that starts its turn in the emanation gain 5 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321), a +1 item bonus to saving throws, and a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) and [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks until the start of your or the ally’s next turn. If you’re [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64) and drink this potion, it fails to work, and you must succeed at a DC 30 Fortitude save or the potion renders you [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 2.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Empath's Cordial](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4616)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 89

**Price** 65 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The pale liquid of _empath’s cordial_ changes color to reflect the mood of the creature nearest to it—turning blue for calm, red for anger, and green for envious, among other hues. For 1 hour after you drink this potion, you can sense the presence of general emotions, such as hostility toward you or the presence of an emotion effect impacting a creature’s emotions, within 30 feet. A creature that failed a saving throw against [_calm emotions_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458) can’t be detected. This potion doesn’t allow you to automatically tell what emotions a specific creature is experiencing, but you can attempt a [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) check with a +2 item bonus (DC set by the GM) to discern that information. The potion also grants you a +1 item bonus to Perception checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) against creatures whose emotions you can sense.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Empath's Cordial

    right="Item 7"

>
[Ensnaring Disk](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4638)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 93

**Price** 70 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You use the affixed shield to [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) a melee weapon attack

**Requirement** You are an expert in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36).
</row>

</column>

---

This coin-sized metal disk is inscribed with arcane symbols and mounted on the inner surface of a shield. When you Activate the disk, the triggering weapon momentarily sticks to your shield, allowing you to attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) it from its wielder with a +2 item bonus. If you roll a critical failure on this check, you get a failure instead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 96 **Price** 140 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact, Strike --- When stared at, a _bewildering spellgun_ seems to warp the space around it, creating a mind-bending sensation. Whispers of gibberish arise from it, making their way to nearby ears despite any other sounds in the area. You Activate the spellgun by aiming it at one creature and making your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target’s AC. This spellgun has a range increment of 15 feet. Once fired, the spellgun twists in your hand and melts away. **Critical Success** The target is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 minute.
**Success** The target is confused for 1 minute but can attempt a DC 25 Will save at the end of each of its turns to end the effect.

    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Amulet of the Third Eye](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4665)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 102

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn amulet

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This large brass medallion hangs low on the torso. It’s shaped in the form of an unblinking eye, with a ring of turquoise as the iris and an orb of jet serving as the pupil. The amulet grants you a +2 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks. When you invest the amulet, you either increase your Wisdom score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever is higher.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Amulet of the Third Eye

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Artificer Spectacles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4667)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 102

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyeglasses

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These seemingly ordinary rectangular eyeglasses feature clear lenses framed in copper. When invested and worn, they rest perfectly on the bridge of the nose and can only be removed by the wearer. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks and any skill check made to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2365). When you invest the spectacles, you either increase your Intelligence score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever is higher. You must select the skills and languages the first time you invest the item, and whenever you invest the same artificer spectacles, you get the same skills and languages you chose the first time.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You cast a 3rd-level [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597) spell on an item you touch.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Artificer Spectacles

You must select the skills and languages the first time you invest the item, and whenever you invest the same artificer spectacles, you get the same skills and languages you chose the first time.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You cast a 3rd-level [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597) spell on an item you touch.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

[Avalanche Boots](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4668)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn boots

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While the insides of these boots are comfortable, fur-lined leather, the outsides are a jumble of slate plates, giving the impression of a rockslide. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks and a +2 circumstance bonus to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) and [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380). When you invest the boots, you either increase your Strength score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever is higher.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You succeed or critically succeed with a [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380);  **Effect** If the Shove was a success, you push your opponent up to 10 feet instead of 5 feet. If the Shove was a critical success, you push your opponent up to 20 feet, and you can then choose to knock them [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

</column>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

These silken handwraps feature intricate embroidery of a serpentine red dragon adorned with golden thread. The handwraps function as _[+3 major striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [greater flaming ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838) [handwraps of mighty blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3086)_. You also gain a +4 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks made to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) or [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380). When you invest the handwraps, you either increase your Strength score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever is higher.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You slap the bottom of your palms with hands splayed outward, casting a 7th-level [_burning hands_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1457) spell (DC 41).

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You succeed or critically succeed with a [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376);  **Effect** You gain a +2 status bonus to your [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) DC against any checks made to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) your [grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) until the end of your next turn.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Laurel of the Empath](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4672)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 103

**Price** 70,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cap

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This silvery, woven ring of leaves sits on top of the head. While wearing it, when you roll [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) for initiative, you can roll twice and take the higher result. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect. Whenever you spend at least 1 minute talking with a living creature, you automatically become aware of its attitude toward you. When you invest the laurel, you either increase your Wisdom score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever is higher.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** You’re hit by an attack;  **Effect** You take half damage from the triggering attack.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You fail, but don’t critically fail, a saving throw;  **Effect** You anticipate the danger and guard against it, often in unconventional or almost inconceivable ways. Treat the failed saving throw as a success.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> (10 minutes) envision ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You spend 10 minutes talking with one living creature, conversing in inspirational, religious, or philosophical terms. You gain valuable insights into the personality of your target—their alignment, hopes, dreams, and fears. When the ritual is over, you gain a +4 item bonus to all [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks made concerning the target for one month. Also, the target gains inspirational insight, allowing the target to use one of the two reactions listed above once during the next 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

[Pilferer's Gloves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4674)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Made of soft and subtle black leather, these gloves fit tightly but aren’t uncomfortable and don’t impede your sense of touch. As long as you’re trained in [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) while wearing these gloves, you’re always considered one skill rank higher than your actual rank. If you possess a Legendary skill rank in Thievery, you gain a +2 item bonus to Thievery checks instead. When you invest the gloves, you either increase your Dexterity score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever is higher.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You fail or critically fail a [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) skill check;  **Effect** If you failed the [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) skill check, you succeed at that check instead. If you critically failed, you fail instead.

</column>

These sturdy leather sandals feature straps that wrap up to the knees. Etched in the leather are intricate patterns of stags leaping through the forest. You gain a +5-foot item bonus to your land Speed and a +3 item bonus on [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks when attempting to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378). When you invest the sandals, you either increase your Strength score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever is higher.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** You attempt a [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378) but you didn’t [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) at least 10 feet;  **Effect** You can attempt the jump normally. It doesn’t automatically fail.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cape

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This black cape flows more like a vaporous liquid than fabric. The cape grants you a +3 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks. When you invest the cape, you either increase your Dexterity score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever is higher.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** A creature misses you with an attack;  **Effect** Attempt a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) check against the triggering creature’s [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) DC. If you roll a success, you’re [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) from that creature until the end of your next turn or until right after you use a [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=80) action against that creature. If you roll a critical success, you’re hidden from that creature until the end of your next turn, even if you use hostile actions against that creature.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** With a twirl of the cape, you transform yourself into a puff of gray smoke. You cast [_gaseous form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1738) on yourself.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Staff" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=151" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 105

**Price** 70,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This gnarled wooden staff is carved with humanoid faces in various emotional states. When the staff is activated, the faces begin to whisper a variety of languages in sibilant tones, creating what seems to be nonsense to all but the staff’s wielder or those they choose to affect. The staff functions as a [_major staff of divination_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3048). While using the staff, you gain a +3 item bonus to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2363), [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2365), and [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) checks, regardless of the skill. When you invest the staff, you either increase your Intelligence score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever is higher. You must select the skills and languages the first time you invest the item, and whenever you invest the same whispering staff, you get the same skills and languages you chose the first time.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** Through the staff’s strange whispering, you gain a glimpse into the mind and desires of one creature you can see within 30 feet. Until the end of your next turn, that creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to you and takes a –2 circumstance penalty to saving throws against your spells.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You point the staff at one creature you can see within 30 feet of you, causing the whispers to howl in that creature’s mind. The target must attempt a DC 43 Will save. If it fails, whenever the creature attempts an attack roll, skill check, or saving throw, it must roll twice and take the worse roll. This lasts until the start of your next turn.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You twirl the staff in three consecutive circles and call for the whispers to speak up. For the next minute, you and all allies within a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) around you can hear your staff’s whispers clearly and distinctly, gaining benefit from their advice and mental protection. Whenever you and your affected allies attempt to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) or attempt a saving throw against a mental effect, you roll twice and take the better result. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
